behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real CLI + real git rename semantics. A mock can hide rename detection nuances (e.g., how git reports renames vs add/delete pairs) and miss subprocess/output parsing issues. Integration was considered, but rejected because behavior text is about actual repository changes as seen by the tool, not only internal categorizer logic. Agentic/Workflow rejected because the behavior contains no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, commit baseline, rename a tracked file, run the CLI as a subprocess against the repo, parse stdout report, and assert: renamed count includes the file, added/deleted counts do not also include that same rename.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Real binary diff behavior often fails at subprocess I/O/encoding boundaries; mocks of git output can always pass and hide decode/crash bugs. This behavior explicitly depends on real git handling of binary content. Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp git repo, add and modify a real binary file (e.g., random bytes), invoke CLI subprocess, assert process exits successfully, and assert report includes the binary file in the appropriate category/count.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This is a real git repo state edge case (unborn HEAD) exposed through CLI/subprocess behavior and git exit/status semantics. In-process stubs can miss actual error codes/stderr handling. Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is purely deterministic CLI/git behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert zero/non-error exit and structured empty report on stdout (all categories empty/zero, no stack trace).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Correctness is defined against actual repository history, so minimum reliable test uses a real repo history and real CLI execution. Integration with mocked git responses could validate arithmetic but not true end-to-end history interpretation. Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI/ML decision quality is present.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a controlled sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifications, run the CLI subprocess over the target range/state, and assert report counts exactly match known ground truth from the constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics, which are OS/git-state interactions at runtime. Mocking git layer can hide discrepancies in how staged/unstaged states are surfaced. Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text has no AI/ML component.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once as staged, run CLI subprocess for each case, normalize non-semantic formatting if needed, and assert outputs are identical.
